title: NOC 22212 – Drafting Technologists and Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Drafting Technologists and Technicians are in strong demand across Canada, fueled by post-pandemic construction growth, infrastructure renewal, advanced manufacturing, and large-scale utility and resource projects. Classified under NOC 22212 (TEER 2), this occupation aligns well with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or apprenticeship normally required

duties: Develop engineering drawings from technical concepts
Create BIM models for buildings & infrastructure
Produce plans, diagrams & layouts from sketches
Operate drafting software or manual stations
employment_requirements: Two- to three-year college diploma in engineering design & drafting technology (technologists)
One- to two-year drafting diploma, apprenticeship, or equivalent experience (technicians)
Around two years of supervised work experience for certification
job_outlook: This competitive outlook favours candidates with:
pathways: Advancement into BIM, design-management, or supervisory roles
additional_information: Canadian employers continue to recruit drafting professionals for:
Construction & consulting firmsManufacturing plantsUtilities & energy companiesMining & resource operationsTransportation & infrastructure agenciesFederal, provincial & municipal governments
Annual salaries typically range between $38,500–$79,800 CAD, depending on province, certification, and technical specialization.
